description_long: This radio receiver features a brushed metal front panel with a horizontal blue-lit frequency display and a classic analog VU meter on the left. The front face is equipped with five rotary knobs, a large tuning knob on the right, and several push buttons, all designed for tactile control. The top surface has two sets of ventilation slits, suggesting internal heat dissipation for electronic components. The sides are finished in a warm wood veneer, giving it a vintage aesthetic. The rear panel includes multiple RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, indicating compatibility with various audio sources and external speakers. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for placement in home garages, workshops, or urban community centers.
description: A vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wood veneer sides, blue-lit frequency display, and multiple control knobs and ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-lit frequency display, analog meter, five control knobs, push buttons, and a large tuning knob.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wood veneer panel and the protruding control knobs on the front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying multiple RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, as well as the metal back panel with ventilation slits.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wood veneer panel and the large tuning knob protruding from the front.
